description:
When bl init runs in a repo whose committed .balls/config.json already declares state_remote (a fresh clone of an already-onboarded client), init adopts it: fetch and track <state_remote>/balls/tasks instead of creating a fresh local orphan. With no state_remote configured, init keeps today's behavior exactly: create-or-track the local balls/tasks (store_init.rs already tracks origin/balls/tasks if present rather than clobbering; preserve that). An unaware bl init in a not-yet-linked client stays safe: it creates an isolated local store (today's behavior), and bl remaster (sibling task) is the documented path to fold those tasks into the project later. Init must never reset, force-push, or overwrite a non-empty balls/tasks it discovers.

WHY: the user's stability bar is "stable if someone runs bl init in the client without knowing there's a main." The answer: unaware init is safe-but-unlinked, and joining is an explicit reconcilable step, never a destructive surprise in either direction.
title: bl init safety: adopt a configured state_remote; never clobber a shared branch
